summary: Ourchival is a private visual-reference archive whose browser clipper and Reliquary web app share a Convex catalog while Google Drive remains the preferred home for original files.

reviews.summary: Initial map established the browser-to-vault capture path, Convex catalog, portable Drive storage, Reliquary experience, shared contracts, and unresolved privacy boundary.

questions.question: What should authenticate the public Convex HTTP capture and vault endpoints before Ourchival is deployed beyond personal development?
questions.whyItMatters: The archive is intended to be private, while the committed HTTP actions currently allow broad cross-origin access without an identity gate.

questions.question: Which canonical identity should prevent the same source asset from producing duplicate references?
questions.whyItMatters: The intended model is one image with many meanings, but reliable capture needs to distinguish reuse from accidental duplication.

questions.question: How prominently should Reliquary distinguish Drive originals, Convex fallbacks, linked assets, and metadata-only captures?
questions.whyItMatters: Storage fallback is useful only when the user can tell what was preserved and what may require recovery.

areas.name: Browser-to-vault capture pipeline
areas.purpose: Turns an image, link, page, or selected context from the browser extension into a source-aware reference and optional stored asset.
areas.verdict: The essential loop is present; deduplication and richer platform parsing are the next reliability boundaries

walkthrough.title: Follow one clipped image into Reliquary
walkthrough.outcome: See where browser context becomes portable source metadata, a Convex record, and—when configured—a private Drive original.

steps.label: Capture the browser context
steps.explanation: The extension converts the clicked image, link, page, and selection into a shared capture payload.
steps.invariant: Every save retains a source URL even when no file can be stored.

steps.label: Send to the configured vault endpoint
steps.explanation: The popup-owned endpoint is normalized and the last capture/result are persisted for visible recovery feedback.
steps.invariant: A missing or malformed endpoint fails visibly rather than silently losing the capture.

steps.label: Store the asset or preserve the link
steps.explanation: The capture action fetches remote bytes when applicable, prefers Drive, falls back when possible, and creates catalog records.
steps.invariant: Reference metadata survives even when remote file retrieval is unavailable.

steps.label: Render the saved reference
steps.explanation: Reliquary polls the Convex site endpoint, renders the appropriate lane, and exposes source and storage context in the inspector.
steps.invariant: The source remains inspectable independently of the stored or proxied image.

areas.name: Convex catalog and relationships
areas.purpose: Stores references, assets, source snapshots, boards, tags, projects, reuse relationships, and export history as the searchable archive catalog.
areas.verdict: A strong domain model that already separates one asset from its many project and board meanings

areas.name: Portable original-file storage
areas.purpose: Keeps originals in private Google Drive when configured while recording Drive identifiers and supporting Convex or linked fallbacks.
areas.verdict: The portability decision is sound; operational status and fallback visibility need to become first-class UI

areas.name: Reliquary vault experience
areas.purpose: Provides dense browsing, lanes, search, favorites, manual capture, editing, source inspection, and the beginnings of boards and project reuse.
areas.verdict: The daily-vault center is visible; boards, projects, and upload remain intentionally incomplete

areas.name: Shared capture and parsing contracts
areas.purpose: Keeps payload types and platform detection reusable between extension, backend, and future site-specific parsers.
areas.verdict: Useful seam with the X/Twitter-specific parser still deliberately ahead

areas.name: Private deployment boundary
areas.purpose: Must eventually gate the vault UI and capture endpoints while keeping Drive files private and development setup usable.
areas.verdict: High-priority missing boundary; the roadmap correctly refuses to imply that the current HTTP surface is private
